sourceMovie.title: Splinter
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Splinter for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Toby Wilkins keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Assimilate
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Splinter clicked for you, Assimilate (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Underwater
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Splinter clicked for you, Underwater (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Splinter?
faq.answer: Try Assimilate, Underwater, Shivers—each shares DNA with Splinter while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Splinter?
faq.answer: Splinter (2008) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Crime, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Splinter worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
